title: Timothée Chalamet wins Best Comedy/Musical Actor at Golden Globes, cements Oscar frontrunner status
text: Analysis of Timothée Chalamet's Golden Globes Win. Timothée Chalamet won Best Actor in a Comedy/Musical at the 2026 Golden Globes for his role in Marty Supreme, a film about a table tennis champion. This victory marks his first Golden Globe win after five previous nominations. Competition & Significance: The win held particular importance because Chalamet competed directly against Leonardo DiCaprio, widely considered his main rival for the upcoming Academy Award. The categorization advantage—with Chalamet headlining Marty Supreme versus DiCaprio in the ensemble cast of One Battle After Another—likely contributed to his victory. Momentum Building: This Golden Globe follows Chalamet's recent Critics Choice Award win, strengthening his position as an Oscar frontrunner. The article notes he could become the second-youngest Best Actor winner ever, having previously lost the Oscar to Adrien Brody for A Complete Unknown. Marketing Success: The film's promotional campaign was notably coordinated, featuring leaked marketing materials, blimps, designer merchandise, and coordinated orange outfits at the premiere.
